ops.title: captain-hook (and consumers) should enable shipped general/python packs via packs.toml, not vendor local .claude/hooks copies — vendoring drifts stale
ops.body:
The user wants captain-hook to **consume the shipped `general`+`python` packs** (enabled by an empty-table entry in `.claude/hooks/packs.toml`: `[packs.general]` / `[packs.python]`) instead of vendoring copies of pack hooks in `.claude/hooks/*.py`. Keep only genuinely repo-specific hooks as a slim local overlay — packs are the baseline; a same-named local hook overrides the pack one.

**Why:** vendored copies drift silently. captain-hook's `.claude/hooks/docs.py` froze at slop-cop `--markdown=on` while the maintained `captain_hook/packs/general/docs.py` had already moved to `--lang=markdown` — that drift is what produced the bogus `uvx slop-cop check … --markdown=on` a session improvised.

**How to apply:** hand-write `packs.toml` (still fine) or use `capt-hook pack add` — since v9.0.0 the pack commands edit ONLY `.claude/hooks/packs.toml`; the old `regenerate_settings` clobber (rewriting settings.json launcher commands to `uvx capt-hook`) is deleted along with `register-hooks` ([[hook-multi-fire-was-multi-source-registration]]). The captain-hook dev repo keeps its `.venv` launcher by disabling its own plugin (`"captain-hook@captain-hook": false`), not via settings hook entries. An empty table = builtin pack (`manager.parse_entry`); `source`/`commit` keys are only for external GitHub packs. captain-hook dogfoods both `general` (commands/docs/plans/prompts/review/stewardship/tasks) and `python` (style/testing/toolchain). Verify with `uv run capt-hook pack list` (counts + drift warning) and `uv run capt-hook test` (runs every hook's inline `tests={}`). slop-cop is a Go binary, never `uvx slop-cop`. Links [[slop-cop-binary-resolution]], [[fix-framework-not-hooks]], [[declarative-capt-hooks]].
